chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: Welcome back to aliens. My name is Navin Reddy and let's continue with the series of Python.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: And in this video, we'll talk about one of my favorite topic is called as data types.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: Now why it's so important because we have worked with variables, right? Everything was

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: working. Now when you work on a project, it's very important to understand what data you're

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: working with so that you can process that data in that format. Because if you don't

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: process the data in that particular format, you might face errors, or maybe bugs and trust

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: me bugs are horrifying. So what are the data types we have here. So let me just list those

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: things here. The first one is none. Second one is numeric. The third one we have is least

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: double, set string, range, and map, or you can call it as dictionary. Now let's start

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: with the first one. The first one is none know what it means. Now when you have a variable

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: and if that variable is not assigned with any value, it is it is none. Normally in other

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: languages, we use a keyword as null. But in Python, we use none. The second one is numeric

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: type. Now if we talk about numeric, we have multiple types here. In fact, we can go with

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: four. The first one is int, then we have float, then we have complex and we have bool. So

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: when we talk about numeric, it works numbers, right? Example, when I say num is equal to

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: 2.5. Now this is float. Okay, so float value. Now how do we know that this is float? In

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: fact, in the last session, we have done that, right? So we can use a type as a function.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: And in this you can pass num and you can say it says class float. Again, we'll talk about

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: class later. Okay, what class means, but time being, it's a float. Okay, so now I will say

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: another number, I will say num is equal to five. Of course, we can change the type of

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: variable, right? So now if I assign five, if I say num, and you can see the class now

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: changed to int, that means the type of this num is int. We also have complex, right? Now

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: what complex means, so I will say num is equal to now if you remember mathematics, we have

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: used to have this concept of complex numbers, right? Now, how do we represent complex numbers?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: So we used to say a plus b j, or in fact, it was i in complex number, but here we have

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: to use j. Now we can replace the value of a with a number, I will say six plus maybe

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: nine j. Now why this j represented by a square root of minus one, okay, now so when I say

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: enter, you can see it is it is accepting the value. And if I see the type of this num,

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: it's your complex type. So complex basically means you have a number plus a plus or minus

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: a imaginary number. Okay, now this one, so we have int, we have float and we have complex.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: So can I convert from a different type example, I have a value which is float and I want to

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: convert that into integer. Is it possible? Let's try. So what I will do here is I will

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: take a variable called as a and the value in a I will assign as 5.6. I have another

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: variable which is B and I want to assign the int value of a can I convert it because if

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I see the type of eight float, right, I want to I want to get the type of B as int and

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I want only I want to have the value which is five. So in that case, you will be using

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: our int function in which you will pass a. So this int function will convert your float

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: value or any value into integer format. And that works. Now if I say the type of B, it's

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: int and the value of B is five, right? So we can convert that. Can I convert int value

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: into float? Yes, we can. So let's say we have another variable which is K. And I want to

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: fetch the value of B, but not in the int format I want to I want in float format. Yes, we

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: can do that. So I can say float and in this bracket, you can pass it and you will get

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: so if I say K, you can see we got 5.0 and of course the type of it is float. You can

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: take and check it out later. Can I convert a normal number into complex number example,

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I have a variable and I have B variable. So we will change the value for K and I will

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: send the K to six. Okay, so we got B as five and K as six. Now I want to get a complex

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: number. So I will say C is equal to how do we get a complex number? It's very simple.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: We just use a complex function that the way we are doing and then you can pass two variables

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: here or one only one parameter that when that works, if I want to pass two parameters, I

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: can pass B comma K. And if I say C, you can see the value now. So it says five plus six

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: J because five is your B and six is your K. Okay, so it works. So we can convert the value

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: from you know, from here to there. In fact, we can we can work with string as well. But

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: we'll see that later. Now we also have a type called as bull, which is also called as Boolean.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: Now how can we use a Boolean value here? Now when it comes to Boolean, it simply means

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: true and false. Now think about this computer world, right? Or in this world, we always

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: take decisions and decisions are based on true or false. And now we in your mind, we

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: always work with these conditions. Example, let's say if you have two choices, you will

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: select those choice which is more favorable or which will be more profitable, right? So

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: we apply conditions there. So if that is more profitable, we'll go there. If this is more

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: profitable, we'll go here. Then the same way we apply a condition and we get true or false.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: So that true or false comes under Boolean type. Now we have B and K, right? So if I

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: want to check if B is less than K, we can do that. So less than symbol is less than

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: and if I say enter, you can see we got true. So true here is a Boolean type, right? And

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: you can assign that to a variable as well. So I will say variable type as bull is equal

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: to B less than K. Now you can see in this bull, if I type bull, you can see it is true.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: And if I say the type of bull is bull itself, because it's a type name as well, right? So

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I should be I should have used a different name here, right? So that's Boolean for you.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: In fact, we have true and we have false as well. Example, if I say B is greater than

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: K, and that's not the case, right? B is 5, K is 6. So it should give you false. So we

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: have true and we have false. Again, there are different operators which we can use less

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: than greater than. So we have different operators which we'll discuss in the upcoming videos.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: But here we can use true or false. But then why I'm keeping true and false in numeric?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: It should be different, right? The thing is, in Python, what we do is we use true as 1

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: and false as 0. Example, let's say if I want to convert true into a number, you can say

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I'm converting them to into informat. And you can see we got one. The same thing if

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: I do for false, you can see we got zero. So true is one and false is zero. So that's your

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: integer format. You know, that's your numeric type. So we have talked about none, we have

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: talked about numeric. The next type we have is in fact, we have discussed those names,

chunks.text: right? Those things comes under sequence. So when you say laced, range, set, tuple,

chunks.text: do we define list is very simple. You will say lst equal to, you can define a list here.

chunks.text: So I can say 25, 26, 45, 12, any number, any number, any format doesn't matter. You can

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python

chunks.text: same thing can be done with set. So if I get a set here, I will say s equal to and you

chunks.text: these values and you can see if I say enter and if I say s, you got the values there and

chunks.text: you can see it is not repeating. And if I define the type of this s, it is set, right?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: So even this works. Now the another type we have is tuple, which we have worked with again.

chunks.text: you can use any format doesn't matter, right? You can see we got t and if I say type of

chunks.text: t, here is tuple. Okay, now we also have a string type, right? So how do we define a

chunks.text: using double quotes or single quotes, both works. I can say my name. That's Navin and

chunks.text: in earlier videos, right? So if you want to know more about list and tuple, you can go

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: of let's say 10 values from zero to nine, we will use a range there. Okay, but you might

chunks.text: be saying, well, what if I want to have a range with different iterations, maybe a different

chunks.text: So range does take three parameters as well. And you can see we got two, four, six, and

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: eight. So I should have went till 11. Right? Because that's the ending part. Okay, so this

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: is how you can use a range. In fact, you can just type here and you can say range, of course,

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: right, it will give you range as you can, it will give you range type. So that's one

chunks.text: type you can use. And all these things comes under sequence, right? Because we are getting

chunks.text: a sequence of values. And now we have one of the most exciting type, which is called

chunks.text: you know, when you have huge amount of data, and even when you want to fetch data in an

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: But in dictionary for every value, we'll assign a key example. Let's say I want to have all

chunks.text: Samsung, maybe Samsung j eight, index number 3, 1 plus 3, index number four, let's say

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: phone has a name or a key attached to it. So it will be easier for you to fetch right?

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: Okay, now it's not just about string. Okay, you can also use numbers you can also use

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: example, roll numbers. Example, when you go for giving your CT exams or entrance exams,

chunks.text: you get a number right? That's your key. So you can identify each object with a key. How

chunks.text: do I do that here? Oh, there's one thing to remember, key should be unique. So if you

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: have a dictionary, all the keys has to be unique. And values you can repeat values.

chunks.title: Python Tutorial for Beginners | Data Types in Python
chunks.text: So let's try it out. How do we define a dictionary? It's very simple. Let's say D is equal to
